# figures/

## tikz/

Eight self-contained `tikzpicture` bodies, each usable with `\input`.

Generated by `verif_v10/v10_maketikz.m` (they carry an
"auto-generated" header; re-run that module to rebuild them):

| file | content |
|---|---|
| `fig_approach.tex` | the optimal inverse temperature rising toward `H` from below, and the oracle excess against the `1/log n` reference |
| `fig_separation.tex` | the two-sided separation on log–log axes, with the `c/sqrt(log n)` and `c/log n` guides |
| `fig_errprob.tex` | excess error probability under 0–1 loss, and the adaptivity ratio for decisions against codelength |

Hand-written explanatory diagrams; every number in them is computed by
`verif_v11/v11_figdata.m`:

| file | content |
|---|---|
| `fig_mechanism.tex` | how the predictor works, on `abracadabra`: match lengths, the votes they cast, and the mass migrating from the order-0 marginal to the longest match as the temperature falls |
| `fig_induction.tex` | the induction head and the soft Mycielski predictor as one computation under two vocabularies |
| `fig_phase.tex` | the order-weight profile in the three phases: decaying, flat, climbing |
| `fig_bridge.tex` | chord subtraction turning the fluctuation path into an endpoint-pinned bridge |
| `fig_m78.tex` | the LZ78 parse, the dictionary trie, and per-order suffix tracking |

Required preamble:

```latex
\usepackage{amsmath,amssymb}
\usepackage{tikz,pgfplots}
\usetikzlibrary{decorations.pathreplacing,arrows.meta,positioning,calc,fit,shapes.geometric}
\pgfplotsset{compat=1.16}
```

`fig_mechanism`, `fig_induction` and `fig_m78` are sized for a
two-column span (about 17.5 cm); `fig_phase` and `fig_bridge` fit a
single column (8.2 cm).

## png/

Octave-rendered versions of the three data figures, produced by
`verif_v10/v10_plots_png.m`. They exist for readers who would rather
drop in an image than compile TikZ. Axis labels are plain ASCII, because
Octave's gnuplot backend cannot reliably render mathematical symbols;
the TikZ versions typeset them properly and are preferred for print.

## checkfig.py

Reports the extent of a compiled figure and any overlapping text:

```sh
python3 checkfig.py figure.pdf 18.19    # second argument: target width in cm
```

Needs `pdftotext` (poppler-utils). Radicals, accents and superscripts
legitimately overlap their operands, so filter for word-on-word
collisions when interpreting the output.
